topics: PCICT 402 Course Type Lab Course Objectives
1. Enable students to gain hands-on experience in measuring temperature
and speed using various instruments like Optical Pyrometer

and Cup anemometer. 2. Equip students with the ability to design and implement temperature measurement instruments and interface them with computers. 3. Provide students with insights into the characteristics of control valves

and the dynamic response of second-order systems Expt. No. Experiments A) Measurement experiments (4 out of 6 experiment is mandatory) 1 Measurement of temperature using Optical Pyrometer. 2 Measurement of Viscosity using red wood viscometer. 3 Measurement of Viscosity using rotational viscometer. 4 Calibration of bourdon pressure gauge using Dead weight pressure gauge tester. 5 Measurement of (1) level using Capacitive transducer (2) speed using Cup anemometer. 6 Study the principle of operation of a gyroscope and sonic range finder B) Design of Industrial instruments (3 out of 5 experiment is mandatory) 7 Design a thermometer using platinum RTD as sensor with the following specification. Temp range

2Volt to 2Volt Also interface the thermometer with computer using any data acquisition system (mandatory experiment) 8 Design a thermometer using NTC thermistor as the sensor with the following specification Course Assessment Method (CIE
Viva and Timely completion of Lab Reports / Record (Continuous Assessment) Internal Examination Total 5 25 20 50 Temperature Range
0V to 1V 9 Design and implement a thermometer (temperature sensing instrument) using solid state temperature sensing device as the sensor with the following specification. Temperature Range
0V to 4V 10 Design and implement a thermometer (temperature sensing instrument) using thermocouples temperature sensing device as the sensor with the following specification. Temperature Range

0V to 4V Reference junction temp- 25oc (room temp) 11 Design and implementation of signal conditioning circuit for conductivity sensor. C) Transmitter and final control element characteristics & second order system characteristics (2 out of 4 experiment is mandatory) 12 Control valve inherent flow characteristics (with and without positioner) 13 Study the characteristics of elements of final control operation. 14 Study the transmitter characteristics of level

and pressure 15 Analysis of dynamic response of second order system. D) Industrial IoT based Experiments (1 out of 4 experiment is mandatory) 16 Design an IoT based temperature controlled automatic fan 17 Upload Temperature and humidity data to thingspeak cloud 18 Retrieve Temperature and humidity data to thingspeak cloud End Semester Examination Marks (ESE

SWAYAM…) Module No. Link ID 1 https://nptel.ac.in/courses/108105064 Continuous Assessment (25 Marks) 1. Preparation and Pre-Lab Work (7 Marks)  Pre-Lab Assignments
Assessment of pre-lab assignments or quizzes that test understanding of the upcoming experiment.  Understanding of Theory
Evaluation based on students’ preparation and understanding of the theoretical background related to the experiments. 2. Conduct of Experiments (7 Marks)  Procedure and Execution
and following safety protocols.  Skill Proficiency
and troubleshooting skills during the experiments.  Teamwork
Collaboration and participation in group experiments. 3. Lab Reports and Record Keeping (6 Marks)  Quality of Reports
completeness and accuracy of lab reports. Proper documentation of experiments
data analysis and conclusions.  Timely Submission
Adhering to deadlines for submitting lab reports/rough record and maintaining a well-organized fair record. 4. Viva Voce (5 Marks)  Oral Examination
results and underlying principles during a viva voce session. Final Marks Averaging

and record are the average of all the specified experiments in the syllabus. Evaluation Pattern for End Semester Examination (50 Marks) 1. Procedure/Preliminary Work/Design/Algorithm (10 Marks)  Procedure Understanding and Description
Clarity in explaining the procedure and understanding each step involved.  Preliminary Work and Planning
Thoroughness in planning and organizing materials/equipment.  Algorithm Development

Correctness and efficiency of the algorithm related to the experiment.  Creativity and logic in algorithm or experimental design. 2. Conduct of Experiment/Execution of Work/Programming (15 Marks)  Setup and Execution
Proper setup and accurate execution of the experiment or programming task. 3. Result with Valid Inference/Quality of Output (10 Marks)  Accuracy of Results
Precision and correctness of the obtained results.  Analysis and Interpretation
Validity of inferences drawn from the experiment or quality of program output. 4. Viva Voce (10 Marks)  Ability to explain the experiment
procedure results and answer related questions  Proficiency in answering questions related to theoretical and practical aspects of the subject. 5. Record (5 Marks)  Completeness
and accuracy of the lab record submitted SEMESTER 6 INSTRUMENTATION AND CONTROL ENGINEERING SEMESTER S6 DISCRETE TIME SIGNAL PROCESSING
